concerns.text: The plan cites 'MeLiDC-shuffled-completo' on HuggingFace as an alternative source for BCC alloys with yield strength. This dataset is typically a text-based material description corpus (MeLiDC) and does not contain structured numerical yield strength values or crystal structure labels required for regression. Relying on this as a fallback for quantitative modeling is infeasible; the plan must identify a verified numerical dataset (e.g., a specific subset of the NIST Materials Data Repository or a verified Kaggle dataset with explicit yield columns) or explicitly state that no verified numerical alternative exists.

The plan states: 'The verified list contains generic BCC parquet files, but their schema must be verified...'. This implies the existence of a 'verified list' of BCC-specific numerical datasets in the project context. If no such verified list exists (as the MPEA DOI is the only primary source cited), the fallback strategy is speculative. The plan must either cite a specific, verified alternative dataset with a URL/DOI that contains yield strength data, or remove the fallback strategy to avoid assuming the availability of data that may not exist.
